question: Compare CDN options for a course-selling SaaS that needs to stream video to learners globally. I need a frank comparison of: (1) Bunny CDN (bunny.net) — pricing model, edge storage, video streaming (HLS), token authentication, custom SSL for arbitrary custom domains, (2) CloudFront — pricing, complexity, integration with S3 + Lambda@Edge, signed URLs/cookies, custom domain SSL, (3) Cloudflare — R2 storage, Stream product, custom domain SSL via SaaS for Customers, (4) Mux — managed video transcoding + delivery, (5) Key consideration: the platform must serve videos branded with each creator's custom domain (e.g. academy.johndoe.com) and issue SSL certs for thousands of creator domains. Which is the best fit and why?
answer:
